speaker: interviewer
text: Irene's-- Opdyke's so-called museum. She has all her honors on these walls. She will describe some of them to us. Irene, how about the one we see over there?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, this is the-- from Israel, when I received the-- the Medal of Valor, you know?

speaker: interviewer
text: The Righteous-- [INTERPOSING VOICES] The Righteous Gentile?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes. I am so very proud of it.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year was that, do you remember?

speaker: interviewee
text: And this is from-- from speaking at the honorary-- I cannot--

speaker: interviewer
text: This plaque is from the Martyrs Memorial--

speaker: interviewee
text: In Los Angeles, there was a big meeting.

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes. Irene, I see you have a whole wall of resolutions.

speaker: interviewee
text: They are from all different towns here, Yorba Linda, Fullerton, Anaheim, Brea. Proclamation-- and, and they, they all were very honored to give me that. And also, I received-- I-- like, City of Fullerton, I am honorary member-- City of Fullerton, they make me a member of--

speaker: interviewer
text: Great honors, absolutely, very deserving, too. That plaque with the key?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, that's from Scranton, Pennsylvania. I was speaking also. And--

speaker: interviewee
text: That is the medal from Israel, Medal of Valor. You know that there's only around 15,000 of those in the whole-- from the whole world.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, as a Righteous Gentile, yes.

speaker: interviewee
text: Above is from Solomon Schechter School. Those children planted a tree for me. They're building Avenue of the Righteous. I have in about three schools-- three schools that they planted the trees. I'm very proud of them.

speaker: interviewee
text: And then this picture is from Florida.

speaker: interviewee
text: From Florida, Woman of Valor. And I received that from UJA, United Jewish Appeal for-- to raise funds for Israel.

speaker: interviewee
text: Here is the County of Los Angeles. I received a resolution from all the County of Los Angeles. And--

speaker: interviewee
text: Below, this is a picture of-- from Holocaust Museum in Washington, DC, when I was in march. And made-- they made it-- my picture with the picture on the wall.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is from UJA because I raise funds that-- and then the others from Rotary Clubs, I spoke also, not for raising funds, but raising consciousness, you know, trying them-- to tell them, hey, get together, you know? We all need you.

speaker: interviewee
text: Then on this side here-- this--

speaker: interviewer
text: One second. OK, the one up there.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yes, that's B'nai B'rith Children's School  in-- in--

speaker: interviewee
text: In Israel. They made it a one room-- it for me, a thank you for--

speaker: interviewer
text: A gift of love, they say.

speaker: interviewee
text: And below, this is for the Courage to Care. I was given the honor to accept that in Los Angeles in there--

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my greatest award, my daughter, Janina Smith. I love her so much. She lives in Portland, Oregon, not very close to me.

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes. Yes, who are these, Irene?

speaker: interviewee
text: Those are my two grandsons, Robert and Ray. On the right is Robert. He is now 10 years old. And--

speaker: interviewee
text: And they-- a picture of-- they are still younger. They are wonderful two guys.

speaker: interviewer
text: Well, Irene, again, we just thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: I know. You have plenty precious treasure here.

speaker: interviewer
text: And we are very, very grateful to you--

speaker: interviewer
text: --for spending the whole day with us.

speaker: interviewee
text: And I am-- I am thankful that you did the job. But I feel so bad that you worked--

speaker: interviewee
text: --so hard. But this is a once in a life--

speaker: interviewer
text: We didn't-- you did the work. [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: Because this is once a lifetime now.

speaker: interviewer
text: But you did the work, Irene. And we thank you for it.
